posts.title: New And Improved Site Up And Running! JJ Watson W/ The Assist
Celtics Draft Grades By Pick
posts.body:
The 2026 NBA Draft is in the books, and Boston walked away with a pair of intriguing combo forwards. Here's how each pick grades out.

#### Round 1, Pick 27 — Chris Cenac Jr. (Grade: ==B-==)

A 19-year-old forward out of Houston, Cenac Jr. averaged ==9.5 PPG== ==7.9 RPG== as a freshman. He profiles as a lanky three-point shooter and switchable defender with real upside — but taking him at 27 felt like a bit of a reach when better-fit options were on the board. The tools are there; the polish isn't yet.

#### Round 2 — Dillon Mitchell (Grade: ==B+==)

The 22-year-old out of Saint John's posted ==8.0 PPG== ==6.3 RPG==. He's a high-energy athlete who attacks the glass, though the jumper and free-throw stroke remain works in progress. Excellent value this late in the draft.

#### The Verdict

Boston came out of the weekend with **two combo forwards** — but arguably ignored their thinnest position. Center depth went unaddressed, and Henri Veesaar was still on the board when the C's made their calls. Solid swings on talent, a questionable read on team need.

posts.title: Bust Out The Brooms: 76ers And Lakers Both Swept!!!
posts.body:
Sometimes the basketball gods give you a perfect weekend. 🧹🧹

The Philadelphia 76ers and the Los Angeles Lakers — **both swept**. Four-and-out for each. There isn't a whole lot of analysis required here, just a moment to soak it in.

Brooms out, hats off, and let the rest of the league take notes. 😏☘️

posts.title: Processing… Celtics Dealt Early Postseason Exit
posts.body:
This one stings. As heavy favorites carrying the third-best championship odds in the league, the Celtics had the 76ers right where they wanted them — up **3–1** in the first round. Then it all came apart.

Boston dominated Games 1 through 4, with Payton Pritchard delivering Game 4 heroics to push the series to the brink. But Philadelphia flipped the switch the moment **Joel Embiid** returned from injury. After sitting the first three games, Embiid was dominant down the stretch, and the supporting cast followed.

- **Tyrese Maxey** — steady, relentless scoring all series.
- **Paul George** — consistent two-way production when it mattered.
- **V.J. Edgecombe** — the rookie was unexpectedly huge.

#### Where Boston Lost It

The role players disappeared at the worst time. Derrick White went quiet, and Nikola Vucevic and Sam Hauser couldn't provide the lift they did all season. And with **Jayson Tatum unavailable for Game 7**, the margin was simply too thin. Three straight losses to close the series, and a championship-caliber season ends in the opening round.

Time to process. Then time to reload.

posts.title: NBA Playoffs Preview: (2) Celtics vs. (7) 76ers
posts.body:
The bracket is set, and the second-seeded Celtics ==56–26== draw the seventh-seeded 76ers ==45–37== in a first-round showdown. Boston opens as a massive **-12.5 favorite** in Game 1 and holds the third-best championship odds in the field.

#### Philadelphia's Problem

Joel Embiid is ruled out for Game 1 with the recurring injury concerns that have shadowed his season, leaving the Sixers leaning on backups Adem Bona and Andre Drummond up front. The offense will run through:

- **Tyrese Maxey** — ==28.3 PPG== the engine of the attack.
- **Paul George** — ==17.3 PPG== in limited games.
- **V.J. Edgecombe** — promising rookie at ==16 PPG== ==5.6 RPG== ==4.2 APG==.

#### Boston's Edge

It starts with **The Jays** — Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown — backed by sixth man Payton Pritchard and a deep frontcourt of Neemias Queta, Nikola Vucevic and Luka Garza. With Philadelphia undermanned in the paint, Boston's depth advantage looms large. Advantage green.

posts.title: The 2025–26 Season: Underrated Contributors
posts.body:
The season is in full swing, so let's shine a light on the names that don't always make the highlight reels but quietly move the needle for the C's.

#### Luka Garza · C · 5th Season

==7.8 PPG== ==4.0 RPG== ==58% FG== ==43% 3PT==

Boston has historically struggled to establish a dominant paint presence outside the Russell and Parish eras. Garza is helping change that. In roughly 16 minutes a night, his per-40 projection is a staggering **25.2 PPG, 9.9 RPG, 2.0 APG**. Toss out the meaningless finale against Orlando with rested starters, and his March–April efficiency has been elite.

#### Jordan Walsh · G · 3rd Season

==5.3 PPG== ==3.9 RPG== ==51.9% FG== ==39.5% 3P==

Walsh has gone from afterthought to genuine rotation piece. His December was outstanding — **9.5 PPG, 4.9 RPG, 1.2 SPG on 69.6% FG, 86.7% FT and 54% from three**. Year over year he's added 23 games, nearly 10 minutes, +15.8% FG, +12.2% 3P and +17.2% FT. That's a leap.

#### Hugo González · G · Rookie

==3.9 PPG== ==3.3 RPG== ==46.7% FG==

The 20-year-old Spaniard debuted for Real Madrid in the ACB at just 16, winning two titles (2024, 2025) before Boston took him 28th in the 2025 Draft. The counting stats are modest, but his March 2nd line against Milwaukee — **18 pts, 16 reb, 3 stl, 2 blk, 3 threes** — flashed exactly why the C's are excited.

#### Honorable Mention: Ron Harper Jr. · G

After torching the G League in Maine (25.2 PPG, 5.1 RPG, 1.2 BPG), Harper has made the most of NBA looks: 22 on San Antonio (six threes), then 27 on Orlando (10-of-20, 7 reb, 4 ast, 3 stl). The undrafted younger brother of 2025 No. 2 pick Dylan Harper plays with a chip on his shoulder. Keep watching.

posts.title: April Showers: Celtics Drain 29 Threes In Blowout
posts.body:
April 10, 2026 was a downpour. The Celtics buried **29 three-pointers** — tying the NBA single-game record — in a ==144–118== demolition of the Pelicans, their 10th straight win.

#### Make It Rain ☔

Sam Hauser led the barrage with **eight threes**. The distribution was absurd — eight different Celtics hit two or more from deep:

- Hauser (8) · Pritchard (5) · Vucevic & White (3) · Brown, Walsh, González & Scheierman (2)
- And the cherry on top: **Neemias Queta drained the first three-pointer of his career.**

#### Jaylen Brown's Big Year

Brown is putting together a career campaign — ==28.8 PPG== (4th in the NBA), ==7 RPG==, ==5.2 APG==. Bona-fide All-Star, and essential to everything Boston does.

#### Tatum Heating Up

Back from injury, JT looks the part. Over his last ten: **22.4 PPG, 10.9 RPG, 6.1 APG**, including a triple-double. With the fourth-best record in the league behind only the Thunder, Spurs and Pistons, Boston is rolling toward the postseason.

posts.title: Happy Easter!!! 🐣🏀💚
posts.body:
Happy Easter from Tatum Time! 🐣☘️

The C's brought a basket full of buckets, beating the Raptors **115–101** on Easter Sunday. Enjoy the day, fam. 💚

posts.title: Jayson Tatum Drops 80 In The First Half!? APRIL FOOLS!
posts.body:
Did Jayson Tatum just drop 80 in the first half? ...April Fools! 🃏 But what actually happened was plenty fun: Boston drenched the Heat ==147–129==.

#### The Dynamic Duo

**Tatum:** a triple-double — 25 points, 18 rebounds, 11 assists, 2 steals.

**Brown:** went off for 43 points on a tidy 17-of-29.

The squad is peaking at the right time heading into the play-in window beginning April 14th. Tatum is rounding back into his optimal self, and this contender is hitting its stride. No joke. ☘️

posts.title: Celtics (50-24) Clinch Playoff Berth For 12th Straight Season
posts.body:
Punch the ticket. With a ==114–99== win over Charlotte, the Celtics ==50–24== officially clinched a playoff berth for the **12th consecutive season**.

Led by Jayson Tatum and anchored by an elite defense — a **106.9 defensive rating, 4th in the NBA** — Boston trails only Detroit in the Eastern Conference standings. The championship window is wide open, and the C's intend to climb through it.

posts.title: Baylor Scheierman Proving His Worth Off The Bench
Celtics Ink Upcoming 20 Year Veteran PG Mike Conley Jr. To One Year Deal
posts.body:
This is a solid move as it adds a veteran floor general to the C's arsenal at PG that already includes the likes of Derrick White and combo guard sharpshooter Payton Pritchard. They got him for pennies on the dollar locking him down for one year at the veteran minimum. At the age of 38, Conley appears to be at the tail end of his career. However, he adds a backcourt presence that is much needed to lead this youthful squad, who have an average age of 25-26 years. His jersey number has yet to be announced.

![](/images/uploads/images.jpg)



![](/images/uploads/hq720.jpg)
